text: Namaskar. Accept greeting from Gastro Institute Kakadev, Kanpur.
I am Dr. Brijendra Singh, consultant surgical gastroenterology.
I am presenting a video laparoscopic cholecystectomy.
The patient is a forty-eight year male who presented with recurrent episode of right upper abdominal pain for last four months. This pain became severe for last few days.
He was clinically examined. He had pulse rate of eighty six per minute. Temperature was normal. His blood pressure was within normal limit. He had tenderness in right upper abdomen.

His investigation showed in ultrasound mild hepatomegaly with grade two fatty liver and presence of gallstones, which is called cholelithiasis. His hemogram was normal. Splitlet count was also within normal limit. His liver function test showed a mild rise in SGPT level. His alkaline phosphatase was within normal limit.
His other clinical examination and chest X-ray ECG were normal.
The surgery is done under general anesthesia.
The surgeon places the initial laparoscopic port through the umbilicus under direct vision, preparing for the cholecystectomy procedure.
The surgeon inserts a 5mm port through the umbilicus and inflates the abdomen with carbon dioxide, preparing the surgical field for laparoscopic cholecystectomy.
Under direct vision, the surgeon places additional ports: a 5mm epigastric port, a port in the right lower quadrant, and a port in the right flank.
The surgeon uses three working channels to grasp and elevate the gallbladder, exposing the critical area known as Calot's triangle.
With the Calot's triangle exposed, the surgeon dissects and coagulates the cystic artery using surgical instruments.
The surgeon isolates the cystic duct, looping it with an absorbable suture, clearly visible within the surgical field.
The surgeon uses a Vicryl suture to ligate the cystic duct, placing a second suture towards the gallbladder site before dividing the cystic duct.
The surgeon uses an electrode to dissect the gallbladder from its bed, simultaneously coagulating and cutting the tissue to separate it from the liver.
The surgical field is suctioned dry, and the gallbladder is extracted through the umbilical port.
The advantage of taking out from cervical protease that is a previous scar present since birth and once the suture is placed in this particular area, there is virtually no scar left.
So this is called virtually scarless laparoscopic cholecystectomy. Thank you for watching.
